items.briefing:
Microsoft has released a major security update patching 622 vulnerabilities, including flaws in SharePoint — a tool widely used in Australian workplaces. If your organisation uses Microsoft products, ensure IT staff apply these patches promptly. Separately, security firm Check Point is reminding organisations that attackers often gain access long before they're detected, meaning strong monitoring and response plans are just as important as prevention.

WatchGuard is also warning about "shadow AI" — staff using unauthorised AI tools without IT knowledge — and weak security habits, both of which increase risk for organisations. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team that Microsoft updates have been applied to all devices this week.

featured.title: Australian critical infrastructure urged to tighten router security after Russian cyber warning
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure operators must urgently secure routers against Russian cyber threats.
